description: Credit-ECOA mirror. The human underwriter's verdict on a referred or contested application. Open this after keystone-credit-decision-v1 returned refer, or when a declined applicant contests (reconsideration). A credit analyst (the performer) assembles the recommendation; an underwriter principal renders the binding approve/decline verdict. The ECOA §1002.9(b)(2) principal-reasons obligation attaches to whatever party actually makes the decision, so on a manual decline the underwriter's principalReasons are load-bearing and are carried into the adverse-action notice. principalReasons are conditional-required on a decline and must be specific ('failed to achieve a qualifying score' is explicitly insufficient under Reg B). Separation of duties is a deployment control: the verdict is rendered by a distinct principal identity (the underwriter agent key), separate from the performer.

decisionRef.description: recordId of the keystone-credit-decision-v1 (disposition=refer) or the contested decision.

principalReasons.description: The specific principal reason(s) for a decline (ECOA §1002.9(b)(2)); ≤4. Conditional-required when verdict=decline.
